lede: A look at December 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Finn Wolfhard and Michael Chopra, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Harmony Jets Flight 185
events.desc: The crash of Harmony Jets Flight 185 killed all eight people on board, including the Libyan Army chief Mohammed al-Haddad in Ankara, Turkey.

events.title: History of Slovenia
events.desc: History of Slovenia: In a referendum, 88.5% of Slovenia's overall electorate vote for independence from Yugoslavia.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 3519
events.desc: After experiencing an engine fire, Aeroflot Flight 3519 attempts to make an emergency landing at Krasnoyarsk International Airport but crashes, killing 110 of the 111 people on board.

events.title: USS Pueblo (AGER-2)
events.desc: The 82 sailors from the USS Pueblo are released after eleven months of internment in North Korea.

events.title: Colombia
events.desc: Colombia becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty.

events.title: Sex Disqualification (Removal) Act 1919
events.desc: Sex Disqualification (Removal) Act 1919 becomes law in the United Kingdom, allowing women to serve as lawyers, civil servants, and in other professions, as well as to serve on juries.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Australian and New Zealand troops arrive in Cairo, Egypt.

events.title: Emma (novel)
events.desc: The novel Emma by Jane Austen is first published.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: George Washington resigns as commander-in-chief of the Continental Army at the Maryland State House in Annapolis, Maryland.

events.title: Yohl Ikʼnal
events.desc: Maya queen Yohl Ik'nal is crowned ruler of Palenque.

deaths.text: G. S. Shivarudrappa, Indian poet and educator
P. V. Narasimha Rao, Indian lawyer and politician, 9th Prime Minister of India
Frederick Tracy Dent, Brigadier General in the Regular United States Army, brother in law to President Ulysses S. Grant.
Johann Sylvan, German theologian (executed; date of birth unknown)

observances.text: Birthday of the Queen Silvia, an official flag flying day (Sweden)
Christian Feast Day: Behnam, Sarah, and the Forty Martyrs (Coptic Church)
